items.original_text: RT @PeterHotez: America is eating its young. We’re a nation built on the greatness of our research universities, it’s what gave us the Manh…
items.zh_text: 转发 @PeterHotez：美国正在吞噬自己的年轻人。我们是一个建立在研究型大学伟大成就之上的国家，正是这些大学给了我们曼哈顿计划、登月计划、互联网、mRNA疫苗、人工智能，以及几乎所有推动我们繁荣与安全的重大突破。如今，我们却通过削减研究经费、限制移民、攻击学术自由来削弱这些机构。这不是在保护美国，而是在摧毁它。

items.original_text: Tired of winning Marc Porter Magee 🎓: MIT announces “the number of grad students will be 20 percent less than it was in 2024 — about 500 fewer students”

items.original_text: i appreciate how seriously the team always takes these reports (even when the answer turns out to be 'i got used to the current level of magic and now i'd like more please') Tibo: Codex team is aware of reports of GPT-5.5 performing worse for some users and investigating. We don't have anything conclusive yet and systems are healthy but we will share updates as we go.
items.zh_text: 我很欣赏团队对这些报告的认真态度（即使最终答案是‘我已经习惯了当前的魔法水平，现在想要更多’）。Tibo：Codex 团队已注意到关于 GPT-5.5 在某些用户中表现更差的报告，并正在调查。我们目前还没有任何结论，系统运行正常，但我们会随时分享更新。

items.original_text: RT ChatGPT A preview for Pro users: a new personal finance experience in ChatGPT. Pro users in the U.S. can securely connect financial accounts, see where their money is going, and ask questions based on the information they choose to connect. Your full financial picture, now in ChatGPT.
items.zh_text: 转发 ChatGPT：面向 Pro 用户的预览版：ChatGPT 中新增个人理财体验。美国 Pro 用户可以安全关联金融账户，查看资金流向，并根据选择关联的信息提问。您的完整财务状况，现在尽在 ChatGPT。

items.original_text: RT Ksenia Se EBM are so back! @ylecun has been pointing here for years: AI reasoning needs systems that check structure before they answer. Aleph from @logic_int now leads the major formal reasoning benchmarks – let me explain what it is -> 📺
items.zh_text: 转发 Ksenia Se：EBM 强势回归！@ylecun 多年来一直指出：AI 推理需要系统在回答之前先检查结构。来自 @logic_int 的 Aleph 现在在主要形式推理基准测试中领先——让我解释一下它是什么 -> 📺

items.original_text: RT Thang Luong Very excited to share a new milestone in AI for Math: Aletheia, powered by Gemini Deep Think, was just used to autonomously solve a Kirby problem! “Kirby’s list” is a “compendium of the most important unsolved problems in topology, the study of deformable shapes” (Quanta magazine). 🧵
items.zh_text: 转发 Thang Luong 非常兴奋地分享人工智能在数学领域的一个新里程碑：由 Gemini Deep Think 驱动的 Aletheia 刚刚被用于自主解决一个 Kirby 问题！“Kirby 列表”是“拓扑学（研究可变形形状的学科）中最重要的未解决问题汇编”（Quanta 杂志）。🧵

items.original_text: Really cool work from the team reimagining the mouse pointer to be intelligent! Try the prototype in @GoogleAIStudio it's pretty magical. Google DeepMind: We’re reimagining a 50-year-old interface - the mouse pointer - with AI. 🖱️ These experimental demos show how people can intuitively direct Gemini on their screens using motion, speech, and natural shorthand to get things done 🧵
items.zh_text: 团队重新构想鼠标指针使其智能化的成果非常酷！在 @GoogleAIStudio 中尝试原型，效果相当神奇。Google DeepMind：我们正在用 AI 重新构想一个已有 50 年历史的界面——鼠标指针。🖱️ 这些实验性演示展示了人们如何通过动作、语音和自然简写直观地引导屏幕上的 Gemini 完成任务 🧵

items.original_text: RT Google Introducing Googlebook, the first laptop designed for Gemini Intelligence. It’s crafted for heavyweight performance, built with Gemini at the core and perfectly synced with your Android phone. Coming this fall. 💻✨ #TheAndroidShow

items.original_text: RT Sundar Pichai Today at the @Android Show (I/O edition) we announced Gemini Intelligence - bringing the best of Gemini to our most advanced devices. Automate multi-step tasks across apps and Chrome, fill out forms in a single tap, turn spoken thoughts into polished text with Rambler, build custom widgets & loads more.
items.zh_text: 转发 Sundar Pichai：今天在 @Android Show（I/O 版）上，我们宣布了 Gemini Intelligence——将 Gemini 的最佳能力带到我们最先进的设备上。跨应用和 Chrome 自动化多步骤任务，一键填写表单，用 Rambler 将口语想法转化为精炼文本，构建自定义小部件以及更多功能。

items.original_text: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc. More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage: 1) raw text (hard/effortful to read) 2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default 3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default ...4,5,6,... n) interactive neural videos/simulations Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444 There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen. TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML. Thariq: http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848
items.zh_text: 顺便说一句，这个方法效果很好：在提问结束时，让你的大语言模型“以 HTML 格式组织回答”，然后在浏览器中查看生成的文件。我还曾成功让大语言模型以幻灯片等形式呈现输出。更广泛地说，我认为音频是人类偏好的 AI 输入方式，而视觉（图像/动画/视频）则是偏好的输出方式。我们大脑中大约三分之一是专门处理视觉的大规模并行处理器，它是信息进入大脑的 10 车道超级高速公路。随着 AI 的进步，我认为我们会看到一系列利用这一优势的演进：1) 纯文本（阅读起来费力/费劲）2) Markdown（粗体、斜体、标题、表格，对眼睛稍微友好一些）<-- 当前默认 3) HTML（仍然有底层代码的程序性，但在图形、布局甚至交互性上灵活得多）<-- 早期但正在形成新的良好默认值 ...4,5,6,... n) 交互式神经视频/模拟。我认为外推（尽管技术尚未存在）最终会走向由扩散神经网络直接生成的某种交互式视频。关于“软件 1.0”产物（例如交互式模拟）如何与神经产物（扩散网格）精确/程序性地交织在一起，还有许多悬而未决的问题，但总体方向类似于最近爆火的 https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444。在输入方面也有必要且待进行的改进。仅靠音频、文本或视频都不够，例如，我觉得需要指向/手势操作屏幕上的内容，就像你与一个物理上在你旁边、一起看电脑屏幕的人所做的一切。简而言之，人类与 AI 之间的输入/输出思维融合正在进行中，还有很多工作要做，并且需要取得重大进展，远在直接跳到 Neuralink 式的脑机接口等之前。在当前阶段值得探索的是，一个小窍门：尝试要求输出 HTML。Thariq: http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848

items.original_text: RT Pushmeet Kohli The future of Math is mathematicians and AI agents working together. Very pleased to introduce @GoogleDeepMind's AI co-mathematician: a multi-agent system designed to actively collaborate with human experts on open-ended research mathematics. Mathematicians testing the agent across areas as diverse as group theory, Hamiltonian systems, and algebraic combinatorics have reported impressive results. In autonomous mode evaluation on the rigorous FrontierMath Tier 4 problems, AI co-mathematician scored an unprecedented 48% — a new high score among all AI systems evaluated.
items.zh_text: RT Pushmeet Kohli 数学的未来是数学家与AI智能体协同工作。非常高兴向大家介绍 @GoogleDeepMind 的AI联合数学家：一个旨在与人类专家在开放式研究数学问题上积极协作的多智能体系统。在群论、哈密顿系统和代数组合学等不同领域测试该智能体的数学家报告了令人印象深刻的结果。在严格的FrontierMath第四级问题的自主模式评估中，AI联合数学家取得了前所未有的48%得分——这是所有被评估AI系统中的最高分。

items.original_text: This is the the quote I've been citing a lot recently. kache: you can outsource your thinking but you cannot outsource your understanding
items.zh_text: 这是我最近经常引用的一句话。kache：你可以外包你的思考，但你不能外包你的理解。

items.original_text: Fireside chat at Sequoia Ascent 2026 from a ~week ago. Some highlights: The first theme I tried to push on is that LLMs are about a lot more than just speeding up what existed before (e.g. coding). Three examples of new horizons: 1. menugen: an app that can be fully engulfed by LLMs, with no classical code needed: input an image, output an image and an LLM can natively do the thing. 2. install .md skills instead of install .sh scripts. Why create a complex Software 1.0 bash script for e.g. installing a piece of software if you can write the installation out in words and say "just show this to your LLM". The LLM is an advanced interpreter of English and can intelligently target installation to your setup, debug everything inline, etc. 3. LLM knowledge bases as an example of something that was *impossible* with classical code because it's computation over unstructured data (knowledge) from arbitrary sources and in arbitrary formats, including simply text articles etc. I pushed on these because in every new paradigm change, the obvious things are always in the realm of speeding up or somehow improving what existed, but here we have examples of functionality that either suddenly perhaps shouldn't even exist (1,2), or was fundamentally not possible before (3). The second (ongoing) theme is trying to explain the pattern of jaggedness in LLMs. How it can be true that a single artifact will simultaneously 1) coherently refactor a 100,000-line code base *and* 2) tell you to walk to the car wash to wash your car. I previously wrote about the source of this as having to do with verifiability of a domain, here I expand on this as having to also do with economics because revenue/TAM dictates what the frontier labs choose to package into training data distributions during RL. You're either in the data distribution (on the rails of the RL circuits) and flying or you're off-roading in the jungle with a machete, in relative terms. Still not 100% satisfied with this, but it's an ongoing struggle to build an accurate model of LLM capabilities if you wish to practically take advantage of their power while avoiding their pitfalls, which brings me to... Last theme is the agent-native economy. The decomposition of products and services into sensors, actuators and logic (split up across all of 1.0/2.0/3.0 computing paradigms), how we can make information maximally legible to LLMs, some words on the quickly emerging agentic engineering and its skill set, related hiring practices, etc., possibly even hints/dreams of fully neural computing handling the vast majority of computation with some help from (classical) CPU coprocessors. Stephanie Zhan: @karpathy and I are back! At @sequoia AI Ascent 2026. And a lot has changed. Last year, he coined “vibe coding”. This year, he’s never felt more behind as a programmer. The big shift: vibe coding raised the floor. Agentic engineering raises the ceiling. We talk about what it
items.zh_text: 安德烈·卡帕西：大约一周前在红杉资本2026年峰会上的一场炉边谈话。一些要点：我试图强调的第一个主题是，LLM远不止是加速已有事物（例如编程）。三个新视野的例子：1. menugen：一个可以完全被LLM吞噬的应用，无需任何传统代码：输入一张图像，输出一张图像，LLM能原生完成这件事。2. 安装.md技能而非安装.sh脚本。为什么要为安装一个软件而创建复杂的Software 1.0 bash脚本，如果你可以用文字描述安装过程并说“只需把这个给你的LLM看”？LLM是英语的高级解释器，能智能地针对你的设置进行安装，内联调试所有问题等。3. LLM知识库，这是传统代码*不可能*实现的例子，因为它涉及对来自任意来源、任意格式的非结构化数据（知识）进行计算，包括简单的文本文章等。我强调这些是因为在每个新范式变革中，显而易见的事情总是加速或改进已有事物，但这里我们有功能示例，要么突然可能不应该存在（1,2），要么以前根本不可能实现（3）。第二个（持续的）主题是试图解释LLM中的锯齿状模式。为什么一个单一工件能同时1）连贯地重构一个10万行代码库*并且*2）告诉你步行去洗车场洗车。我之前写过这源于领域的可验证性，这里我扩展为也与经济学有关，因为收入/总可寻址市场决定了前沿实验室在强化学习期间选择将什么打包到训练数据分布中。你要么在数据分布中（在强化学习电路的轨道上）飞行，要么相对而言在丛林中用砍刀越野。对此我仍不完全满意，但如果你希望实际利用LLM的力量同时避免其陷阱，这是一个持续构建LLM能力准确模型的斗争，这引出了……最后一个主题是智能体原生经济。将产品和服务分解为传感器、执行器和逻辑（分布在所有1.0/2.0/3.0计算范式中），我们如何使信息对LLM最大程度可读，一些关于快速兴起的智能体工程及其技能集、相关招聘实践的讨论，甚至可能暗示/梦想完全神经计算在（传统）CPU协处理器的一些帮助下处理绝大多数计算。斯蒂芬妮·詹：@karpathy 和我回来了！在 @sequoia AI Ascent 2026。很多已经改变。去年，他创造了“氛围编程”一词。今年，他作为程序员从未感到如此落后。重大转变：氛围编程提高了下限。智能体工程提高了上限。我们讨论了这意味着什么。

items.original_text: RT Nick Levine New work with @AlecRad and @DavidDuvenaud: Have you ever dreamed of talking to someone from the past? Introducing talkie, a 13B model trained only on pre-1931 text. Vintage models should help us to understand how LMs generalize (e.g., can we teach talkie to code?). Thread:
items.zh_text: 转推 Nick Levine：与 @AlecRad 和 @DavidDuvenaud 的新工作：你是否曾梦想过与过去的人对话？介绍 talkie，一个仅基于 1931 年前文本训练的 13B 模型。复古模型应有助于我们理解语言模型如何泛化（例如，我们能否教会 talkie 编程？）。线程：

items.original_text: RT Zain Shah Imagine every pixel on your screen, streamed live directly from a model. No HTML, no layout engine, no code. Just exactly what you want to see. @eddiejiao_obj, @drewocarr and I built a prototype to see how this could actually work, and set out to make it real. We're calling it Flipbook. (1/5)
items.zh_text: 转发 @Zain Shah：想象一下，屏幕上的每一个像素都直接从模型实时流式传输。没有 HTML，没有布局引擎，没有代码。只有你确切想看到的内容。我和 @eddiejiao_obj、@drewocarr 构建了一个原型，来探索这如何实际运作，并着手将其变为现实。我们称之为 Flipbook。（1/5）
